edges.description: Loss of DHCR7 blocks consumption of 7-dehydrocholesterol.
Loss of DHCR7 lowers endogenous cholesterol synthesis.
DHCR7-driven sterol imbalance is associated with prenatal growth restriction as a core clinical characteristic.
DHCR7-driven sterol imbalance is associated with postnatal growth restriction as a core clinical characteristic.
Excess 7-dehydrocholesterol provides substrate for formation of reactive oxysterol metabolites.
High cellular 7-dehydrocholesterol burden is linked to oxidative-stress-associated autophagy.
Elevated serum or tissue 7-dehydrocholesterol is the diagnostic biochemical readout of the accumulated precursor pool.
Cholesterol deficiency reduces sterol-dependent Smoothened activation within Hedgehog signaling.
Impaired terminal cholesterol biosynthesis is reflected clinically by low plasma cholesterol.
Reduced cholesterol-dependent Smoothened activation is a mechanistic route from sterol imbalance to SHH-linked congenital malformations.
The sterol-linked external malformation pattern includes classic 2-3 toe syndactyly.
The sterol-linked external malformation pattern includes orofacial clefting.
The sterol-linked external malformation pattern includes postaxial limb patterning defects.
The sterol-linked external malformation pattern includes male external genital underdevelopment.
Oxysterol exposure accelerates neuronal differentiation and perturbs normal cortical development.
Premature neurogenesis and cortical precursor dysregulation provide a mechanistic branch for intellectual impairment in SLOS.
Premature cortical neurogenesis and depletion of precursor pools provide a developmental route to reduced head and brain growth.

edges.source: Avoidance of 7-DHC-elevating medications and extended sun exposure

edges.description: Elevated serum or tissue 7-dehydrocholesterol reports accumulation of the DHCR7 substrate upstream of toxic oxysterol generation.
Low plasma cholesterol reports the cholesterol-deficiency branch of the terminal DHCR7 sterol-biosynthesis block.

nodes.description: Cutaneous syndactyly of the second and third toes is a classic external malformation in SLOS.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: External malformations include distinctive facial features, cleft palate, postaxial polydactyly, 2-3 syndactyly of the toes, and underdeveloped external genitalia in males, while internal anomalies may affect every organ system.

nodes.description: Oxidative metabolites generated from excess 7-dehydrocholesterol perturb neural development and add toxicity beyond cholesterol deficiency alone.

evidence.reference_title: DHCEO accumulation is a critical mediator of pathophysiology in a Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome model.
evidence.snippet: The overall results suggest that 7-DHC oxidative metabolites are critical contributors to altered neural development in SLOS.

nodes.description: 7-Dehydrocholesterol accumulates in plasma and tissues because the final reductive step of cholesterol synthesis is blocked.

evidence.reference_title: Markedly increased tissue concentrations of 7-dehydrocholesterol combined with low levels of cholesterol are characteristic of the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized biochemically by low plasma cholesterol and greatly elevated levels of two dehydrocholesterols, one of which is the cholesterol precursor 7-dehydrocholesterol.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: The clinical diagnosis of SLOS is confirmed by demonstrating an abnormally elevated concentration of the cholesterol precursor, 7DHC, in serum or other tissues, or by the presence of two DHCR7 mutations.

nodes.description: Management includes avoiding haloperidol-class drugs, using caution with psychotropics such as trazodone or aripiprazole that elevate 7DHC, and avoiding extended sun exposure.
nodes.id: Avoidance of 7-DHC-elevating medications and extended sun exposure

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Agents/circumstances to avoid: Treatment with haloperidol or other drugs in the same class. Psychotropic drugs (trazodone, aripirazole) that elevate 7-DHC should be used with caution; extended sun exposure should be avoided.

nodes.description: Pilot bile-acid replacement with cholic acid increased plasma cholesterol in a small SLOS cohort, but current evidence is biochemical and preliminary.

evidence.reference_title: Cholic acid increases plasma cholesterol in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: A pilot study.
evidence.snippet: In this pilot study, cholic acid supplementation was well tolerated and safe and resulted in an increase in plasma cholesterol in most SLOS subjects.

nodes.description: Orofacial clefting is part of the external malformation spectrum in SLOS.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: External malformations include distinctive facial features, cleft palate, postaxial polydactyly, 2-3 syndactyly of the toes, and underdeveloped external genitalia in males, while internal anomalies may affect every organ system.

nodes.description: Embryonic cholesterol deficiency and 7DHC excess disrupt sterol-dependent morphogenesis, producing the characteristic external malformation pattern of SLOS.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: The malformations found in SLOS may result from decreased cholesterol, increased 7DHC or a combination of these two factors.

evidence.reference_title: Reduced cholesterol levels impair Smoothened activation in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Morphological abnormalities in SLOS resemble those seen in congenital Sonic Hedgehog (SHH)-deficient conditions, leading to the proposal that the pathogenesis of SLOS is mediated by aberrant SHH signalling.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the Delta7-sterol reductase gene in patients with the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Our results strongly suggest that defects in the DHCR7 gene cause the SLOS.

evidence.reference_title: Age and origin of major Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome (SLOS) mutations in European populations.
evidence.snippet: In 263 SLOS patients 10 common alleles (c.964-1G>C, p.Trp151X, p.Thr93Met, p.Val326Leu, p.Arg352Trp, p.Arg404Cys, p.Phe302Leu, p.Leu157Pro, p.Gly410Ser, p.Arg445Gln) were found to constitute approximately 80% of disease-causing mutations.

evidence.reference_title: DHCR7 / Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: DHCR7 | HGNC:2860 | Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome | MONDO:0010035 | AR | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: A c.89G>C p.(Gly30Ala) Variant in the DHCR7 Gene as a Cause of a Mild Phenotype in the Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The two unique patients presented here suggest that the c.89G>C p.(Gly30Ala) variant is associated with a mild SLOS phenotype, which may be overlooked and unrecognized in clinical practice.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic DHCR7 variants reduce 7-dehydrocholesterol reductase activity, impairing the terminal conversion of 7-dehydrocholesterol to cholesterol.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the Delta7-sterol reductase gene in patients with the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Here we identify the SLOS gene as a Delta7-sterol reductase (DHCR7, EC 1.3.1. 21) required for the de novo biosynthesis of cholesterol.

nodes.description: Dietary cholesterol supplementation can raise plasma cholesterol and lower circulating 7DHC over time, although it does not fully correct all disease manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Cholesterol supplementation with egg yolk increases plasma cholesterol and decreases plasma 7-dehydrocholesterol in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These results support the hypothesis that over time dietary cholesterol supplementation from egg yolk increases the plasma cholesterol levels and decreases levels of 7-DHC which may be toxic.

nodes.description: Reduced cholesterol availability impairs sterol-sensitive Smoothened activation and its trafficking to the primary cilium.

evidence.reference_title: Reduced cholesterol levels impair Smoothened activation in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Our results indicate that a deficit in cholesterol, as opposed to an accumulation of 7DHC, impairs SMO activation and its localization to the primary cilium.

nodes.description: Cognitive impairment ranges from mild to severe across the SLOS severity spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth retardation, microcephaly, a variable degree of intellectual disability that encompasses normal intelligence to severe intellectual deficiency, and multiple major and minor malformations.

nodes.description: Reduced head circumference is a common growth and neurodevelopmental manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth retardation, microcephaly, a variable degree of intellectual disability that encompasses normal intelligence to severe intellectual deficiency, and multiple major and minor malformations.

nodes.description: SLOS fibroblasts show increased autophagy together with oxidative stress and mitochondrial dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated Autophagy and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in the Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Further, the increased basal LC3B-II levels were decreased significantly by pretreating the cells with antioxidants implicating a role for oxidative stress in elevating autophagy in SLOS cells.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated Autophagy and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in the Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Considering the possible source of oxidative stress, we examined mitochondrial function in the SLOS cells using JC-1 assay and found significant mitochondrial dysfunction compared to mitochondria in control cells.

evidence.reference_title: Markedly increased tissue concentrations of 7-dehydrocholesterol combined with low levels of cholesterol are characteristic of the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized biochemically by low plasma cholesterol and greatly elevated levels of two dehydrocholesterols, one of which is the cholesterol precursor 7-dehydrocholesterol.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: The enzymatic deficiency results in decreased cholesterol and increased 7DHC levels, both during embryonic development and after birth.

readouts.interpretation: Low plasma cholesterol reports the cholesterol-deficiency branch of the terminal DHCR7 sterol-biosynthesis block.

nodes.description: Postaxial extra digits occur in a subset of affected individuals as part of the congenital malformation pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: External malformations include distinctive facial features, cleft palate, postaxial polydactyly, 2-3 syndactyly of the toes, and underdeveloped external genitalia in males, while internal anomalies may affect every organ system.

nodes.description: Growth restriction continues after birth as part of the SLOS growth phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth restriction, microcephaly, moderate-to-severe intellectual disability, and multiple major and minor malformations.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth retardation, microcephaly, a variable degree of intellectual disability that encompasses normal intelligence to severe intellectual deficiency, and multiple major and minor malformations.

nodes.description: Cortical neuronal differentiation occurs prematurely, depleting precursor pools and distorting normal neural development.

evidence.reference_title: DHCEO accumulation is a critical mediator of pathophysiology in a Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome model.
evidence.snippet: We also report that neurons are ten fold more susceptible to a 7-DHC-derived oxysterol mixture than glial cells, and that DHCEO accelerates differentiation and arborization of cortical neurons.

evidence.reference_title: 7-Dehydrocholesterol-derived oxysterols cause neurogenic defects in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Either loss of DHCR7 or direct exposure to DHCEO causes hyperactivation of GR and TrkB and their downstream MEK-ERK-C/EBP signaling pathway in cortical neural precursors.

evidence.reference_title: 7-Dehydrocholesterol-derived oxysterols cause neurogenic defects in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that loss of DHCR7 causes accumulation of 7-DHC-derived oxysterol metabolites, premature neurogenesis from murine or human cortical neural precursors, and depletion of the cortical precursor pool, both in vitro and in vivo.

nodes.description: Fetal growth restriction is a core growth manifestation in SLOS.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth restriction, microcephaly, moderate-to-severe intellectual disability, and multiple major and minor malformations.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by prenatal and postnatal growth retardation, microcephaly, a variable degree of intellectual disability that encompasses normal intelligence to severe intellectual deficiency, and multiple major and minor malformations.

nodes.description: Cholesterol levels fall in plasma and tissues because terminal sterol biosynthesis is impaired.

evidence.reference_title: Markedly increased tissue concentrations of 7-dehydrocholesterol combined with low levels of cholesterol are characteristic of the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These results suggest that a defect in 3 beta-hydroxysterol delta 7-reductase leads to both a profound lack of cholesterol and its replacement by dehydrocholesterols.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: The clinical diagnosis of SLOS is confirmed by demonstrating an abnormally elevated concentration of the cholesterol precursor, 7DHC, in serum or other tissues, or by the presence of two DHCR7 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Markedly increased tissue concentrations of 7-dehydrocholesterol combined with low levels of cholesterol are characteristic of the Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized biochemically by low plasma cholesterol and greatly elevated levels of two dehydrocholesterols, one of which is the cholesterol precursor 7-dehydrocholesterol.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated serum or tissue 7-dehydrocholesterol reports accumulation of the DHCR7 substrate upstream of toxic oxysterol generation.

nodes.description: Adjunctive simvastatin can improve the serum dehydrocholesterol-to-total sterol ratio and irritability in mild to classic SLOS with residual DHCR7 activity.

evidence.reference_title: A placebo-controlled trial of simvastatin therapy in Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Simvastatin seems to be relatively safe in patients with SLOS, improves the serum dehydrocholesterol-to-total sterol ratio, and significantly improves irritability symptoms in patients with mild to classic SLOS.

nodes.description: Male genital underdevelopment is a characteristic external malformation in affected males.

evidence.reference_title: Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome: phenotype, natural history, and epidemiology.
evidence.snippet: External malformations include distinctive facial features, cleft palate, postaxial polydactyly, 2-3 syndactyly of the toes, and underdeveloped external genitalia in males, while internal anomalies may affect every organ system.
